title: Alkenes
summary: 9701 AS alkenes — electrophilic addition, Markovnikov's rule, and polymerisation with PhET Build a Molecule.
learningObjectives: Describe the structure of alkenes and the reactivity of the C=C bond.
Explain electrophilic addition mechanisms with curly arrows.
Apply Markovnikov's rule to predict major products.

simpleExplanation.title: Double bond reactivity
simpleExplanation.summary: The π bond in alkenes is electron-rich and attacked by electrophiles. Addition breaks the π bond and forms two new σ bonds.
steps: Alkenes have formula CₙH₂ₙ with at least one C=C double bond.
Electrophilic addition: E⁺ attacks π bond; carbocation intermediate (may rearrange).
Markovnikov: H attaches to C with more H atoms; X to C with fewer.
Polymerisation: many monomer units add to form poly(ethene), poly(propene), etc.

sections.content: **Alkenes** (14.2) are the gateway to **organic mechanisms** on 9701. Cambridge expects **curly arrow** mechanisms for HBr addition and **Markovnikov** product prediction.

sections.question: State the major product when propene reacts with HBr.
sections.solution:
Mechanism: H⁺ adds first → secondary carbocation at CH⁺(CH₃)₂ (more stable than primary).

Br⁻ attacks → **2-bromopropane** (major, Markovnikov product).

sections.content: Draw **partial charges** on the double bond (C=C δ−) and show **curly arrows** from bond to atom. One curly arrow per step minimum for mechanism marks.
